title: Fitting an under-door draught excluder
subtitle: Threshold seals and brush strips for external doors
excerpt: The gap under an external door is typically 5-15 mm and accounts for a large share of a home's air infiltration. A threshold seal or brush strip reduces this to near zero without impeding the door swing.
sourceNotes: Energy Saving Trust draught-proofing guidance for air infiltration figures. AECB Building Performance Primer on door sealing.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sealing gaps around doors, windows, and penetrations to prevent uncontrolled air infiltration. The cheapest and fastest-payback retrofit in most UK homes.

content.text: The gap under an external door allows cold air in at floor level and warm air out at mid-height. On a windy day the flow can be felt clearly from a metre away.
the threshold is the fastest-payback draught fix in most UK homes, and the installation takes under an hour per door.

Close the door and measure the clearance between the door bottom and the threshold in three places: the latch side, the centre, and the hinge side. If the gap varies by more than 3 mm across the width, the door may be dropped or twisted. A dropped door needs the hinge screws retightened before fitting a threshold seal; a twisted door needs a rebate-type seal that can accommodate the variation.
Brush strip screwed to the door bottom

A 25 mm aluminium extrusion with a polypropylene brush insert screwed across the bottom of the door leaf. The brush compresses against the threshold as the door closes and springs back as it opens, so the door swings freely over a carpet or mat. Suited to gaps of 5-20 mm. This is the most versatile and durable option for most external doors.

A bar fixed to the threshold with a rubber or neoprene insert that the door closes against. The door closes onto the bar rather than onto the floor. Suited to consistent gaps of 5-10 mm on a level threshold. Not suitable where the threshold is uneven or where the door must clear a thick doormat.

Self-adhesive foam compresses well but wears within a year on a frequently used door. Reserve this for internal doors between heated and unheated spaces where the door is used less frequently. For external doors, a screwed or mechanically fixed seal outlasts foam by several years.
Measure the door width at the bottom: outside edge to outside edge, not the frame opening. Cut the brush strip to this length with a utility knife or hacksaw.

Close the door. Hold the brush strip against the bottom of the door with the brush pointing downward. The brush should just make contact with the threshold without lifting the door. Mark the screw hole positions on the door with a pencil.
Drill pilot holes at the marked positions. Pre-drill to avoid splitting the door stile. For a standard 40 mm external door stile, a 2 mm pilot hole is enough for a No. 8 screw.
Screw the strip in place. Open the door, check the brush drags lightly on the threshold, and check the door closes without the brush lifting the door off the latch.
Internal door between heated and unheated spaces

The same approach works on internal doors between a heated space and an unheated one. This includes an integral garage door, an unheated utility room, or a cellar stair. Use a self-adhesive foam strip here. These doors open less often, so foam lasts several years. The fit is easy to reverse if the door needs rehanging.

attrs.symptom: Door catches on the brush strip when opening inward
attrs.cause: Brush strip fitted too low, or brush compression is too high
attrs.fix: Raise the strip by 2-3 mm. The brush should make light contact when closed, not hard contact. Slot-head adjustment screws on some models allow height adjustment without removing the strip.

attrs.symptom: Cold air still detectable after fitting
attrs.cause: Sides or top of door also leaking
attrs.fix: A threshold seal addresses the bottom gap only. If cold air is still felt with the door closed, check the head seal and the side rebates with a lit stick of incense. The top and sides are typically sealed with compressible foam or brush tape in the rebate.
